name: Palestine (State of Palestine / دولة فلسطين)
summary: Palestine (State of Palestine) is a Levantine partially-recognised mixed-legal-system framework operating across the West Bank (under Palestinian Authority administration) and the Gaza Strip with overlapping Ottoman, British Mandate, Jordanian, Egyptian, Israeli military-order, and Palestinian Basic Law legal-system layers. Family-law framework operates under the Jordanian Personal Status Law 1976 (residual application in West Bank under Palestinian Authority) and the Egyptian Family Status Law 1954 (Gaza Strip residual) — both drawing on Hanafi Sunni Islamic jurisprudence — supplemented by Palestinian Authority laws including Law 7/2008 on Family Protection from Violence. Custody (hadana) and guardianship (wilaya) are governed by Jordanian Personal Status Law 1976 arts. 154-180 (West Bank) and Egyptian Family Status Law arts. 25-45 (Gaza). Sharia Courts operate at first instance for Muslim personal-status matters; ecclesiastical/religious courts operate for Christian and Druze communities. The Palestinian Authority High Court of Justice operates apex jurisdiction within the Palestinian Authority framework. Psychology profession is regulated through the Palestinian Ministry of Health framework. Palestine is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Palestine is not a Hague Convention 1980 party. Palestine is a UN observer state (recognised by 138+ UN member states).

statutory_framework.citation: Jordanian Personal Status Law 1976 arts. 154-180 (West Bank residual application)
statutory_framework.title: Jordanian Personal Status Law — Custody and guardianship (West Bank)
statutory_framework.relevance: Jordanian Personal Status Law applied residually in West Bank under Palestinian Authority. Arts. 154-180 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship).

statutory_framework.citation: Egyptian Family Status Law 1954 (Gaza Strip residual)
statutory_framework.title: Egyptian Family Status Law (Gaza residual)
statutory_framework.relevance: Egyptian Family Status Law applied residually in Gaza Strip. Arts. 25-45 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship).

statutory_framework.citation: Palestinian Authority Law 7/2008 on Family Protection from Violence
statutory_framework.title: Law on Family Protection from Violence
statutory_framework.relevance: Palestinian Authority law on family violence prevention affecting family-law proceedings.

statutory_framework.title: Palestinian Basic Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional framework establishing Palestinian Authority institutional structure.

apex_courts.name: Palestinian Authority High Court of Justice
apex_courts.role: Apex court within Palestinian Authority framework for civil and administrative matters.

apex_courts.name: Sharia Court of Appeals
apex_courts.role: Apex Sharia jurisdiction over Muslim personal-status matters.

professional_regulators.name: Palestinian Ministry of Health
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Palestinian family-court decisions are anonymised per Sharia Court of Appeals practice using initials.

key_developments.title: British Mandate of Palestine + League-of-Nations-Mandate framework
key_developments.description: British Mandate of Palestine established 24 July 1922 by League of Nations — substantively distinctive Levantine British-Mandate-+-Balfour-Declaration-framework framework. Foundational substantive 26-year British-Mandate framework persisting through 1948 termination affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and multi-layer-mixed-legal-system framework.

key_developments.title: Nakba + Palestinian-displacement + Israeli-state-establishment-+-Jordanian-West-Bank-+-Egyptian-Gaza-administration framework
key_developments.description: Nakba (Palestinian Catastrophe) May 1948 + Israeli state establishment 14 May 1948 + Jordanian West Bank administration + Egyptian Gaza administration — substantively distinctive globally Palestinian-displacement-+-multi-layer-legal-system-fragmentation framework. Foundational substantive demographic + family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory. ~700,000-750,000 Palestinians displaced.

key_developments.title: Egyptian Family Status Law 1954 + Gaza-residual-application framework
key_developments.description: Egyptian Family Status Law 1954 applied in Gaza Strip under Egyptian administration — substantively significant Levantine Egyptian-Family-Status-Law-Gaza-residual framework. Arts. 25-45 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship). Foundational substantive Gaza-family-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Six-Day War + Israeli-military-occupation-+-West-Bank-+-Gaza-+-East-Jerusalem framework
key_developments.description: Six-Day War 5-10 June 1967 + Israeli military occupation of West Bank + Gaza + East Jerusalem — substantively distinctive Levantine Israeli-military-order-legal-system-layer framework. Foundational substantive 59-year Israeli-military-occupation framework persisting through 21st century affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Jordanian Personal Status Law 1976 + West-Bank-residual-application framework
key_developments.description: Jordanian Personal Status Law 1976 applied in West Bank under Jordanian administration — substantively significant Levantine Jordanian-Personal-Status-Law-West-Bank-residual framework drawing on Hanafi Sunni Islamic jurisprudence. Arts. 154-180 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship). Foundational substantive West-Bank-family-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Oslo Accords + Palestinian-Authority-establishment framework
key_developments.description: Oslo Accords (Declaration of Principles 13 September 1993) + Oslo II Interim Agreement 28 September 1995 established Palestinian Authority with partial civil-administrative jurisdiction in West Bank and Gaza — substantively distinctive globally Palestinian-Authority-partial-civil-administrative-jurisdiction framework. Foundational substantive 33-year Palestinian-Authority framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Palestinian Basic Law + constitutional-framework framework
key_developments.description: Palestinian Basic Law 2003 enacted establishing Palestinian Authority institutional structure — substantively significant Levantine Palestinian-Authority-constitutional-anchor framework. Foundational substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Law on Family Protection from Violence + Law-7/2008 framework
key_developments.description: Palestinian Authority Law 7/2008 on Family Protection from Violence enacted establishing family violence prevention framework — substantively significant Levantine family-protection-from-violence framework. Substantive family-law-implementation framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: UN observer state status + UNGA-Resolution-67/19-+-138-recognising-states framework
key_developments.description: Palestine recognised as UN observer state by General Assembly Resolution 67/19 of 29 November 2012 — substantively distinctive globally partial-recognition-+-138-UN-member-states-recognition framework. Substantive partial-recognition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Palestinian Independent Commission for Human Rights + Sharia Court of Appeal framework + UNRWA family-law coordination
key_developments.description: Palestinian Independent Commission for Human Rights (ICHR) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Personal Status Law 1976 (West Bank) + Personal Status Law 1954 (Gaza) + Sharia Court of Appeal framework. UNRWA family-law coordination framework substantively significant for refugee family-law adjudication across West Bank/Gaza/Lebanon/Syria/Jordan camps. Substantively significant Levant institutional context establishing trajectory toward 2024 Gaza war substantive register.

key_developments.title: Gaza-war-2023-+-ICJ-South-Africa-v-Israel + Palestinian-Authority-Hamas-Gaza-administration context framework
key_developments.description: 2023-2024 Gaza war following 7 October 2023 Hamas attack on Israel + ICJ South Africa v Israel proceedings + 19 July 2024 ICJ Advisory Opinion on Israeli Settlements Wall + 2024 UNGA Resolution 79/3 (Israeli withdrawal demand) — substantively distinctive globally ongoing-armed-conflict-+-ICJ-proceedings-context framework. Substantive demographic + family-law-implementation disruption + apex-international-court-context affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

structural_findings: Palestine operates the most structurally distinctive multi-layer mixed-legal-system framework within the corpus — Ottoman + British Mandate + Jordanian (West Bank) + Egyptian (Gaza) + Israeli military-order + Palestinian Authority Basic Law/Statutes + Hanafi Sunni Islamic jurisdiction + Christian/Druze ecclesiastical jurisdiction. Most layered legal-system framework in the corpus.

Geographic legal-system fragmentation (West Bank Jordanian-derivative + Gaza Egyptian-derivative) is structurally distinctive — single state with two distinct personal-status frameworks based on prior occupier inheritance.
Partially-recognised UN observer state status places Palestine in the disputed-status cluster with Kosovo.
Non-Hague Convention status places Palestine in the non-Hague Levantine cluster.
editorial_notes: Palestine jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 6 to 10 key_developments with full British-Mandate-1922-to-Gaza-war-2023-2024-+-ICJ-proceedings trajectory: 1922-British-Mandate-of-Palestine + 1948-Nakba-+-Israeli-state-+-Jordanian-West-Bank-+-Egyptian-Gaza + 1954-Egyptian-Family-Status-Law-Gaza + 1967-Six-Day-War-+-Israeli-military-occupation + 1976-Jordanian-Personal-Status-Law-West-Bank + 1993-Oslo-Accords-+-Palestinian-Authority + 2003-Palestinian-Basic-Law + 2008-Law-on-Family-Protection-from-Violence-7/2008 + 2012-UN-observer-state-status + 2024-Gaza-war-+-ICJ-South-Africa-v-Israel.

Multi-layer mixed-legal-system Levantine (Jordanian-Personal-Status-West-Bank + Egyptian-Family-Status-Gaza + Palestinian Authority Basic Law + Hanafi Sunni Islamic + ecclesiastical communities + non-Hague). Most layered legal-system in corpus (Ottoman + British Mandate + Jordanian (West Bank) + Egyptian (Gaza) + Israeli military-order + Palestinian Authority Basic Law/Statutes + Hanafi Sunni Islamic + Christian/Druze ecclesiastical jurisdiction). UN observer state recognised by 138+ UN member states.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive hadana + wilaya analysis under Jordanian Personal Status Law 1976 arts. 154-180 (West Bank) + Egyptian Family Status Law 1954 arts. 25-45 (Gaza) + Palestinian Authority Law 7/2008 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.
